item.title: #2547493: Add support for unique / primary key constraints composed of multiple fields for Upsert queries
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +118/−19 · php +118/−19
  • Diff: 3ae3915
  • Discussion: #2547493 · 15 contributors · 58 comments
  • Followers: 22

Summary

Drupal's Upsert database query class now supports composite unique or primary key constraints, meaning tables whose uniqueness is defined by more than one column can now use upsert queries. Previously, the class was limited to single-field keys, which prevented it from being used across most of Drupal core. This unblocks replacing slower multi-statement Merge queries with single-statement Upsert queries in more places, reducing database round-trips. Site owners should see a modest performance improvement wherever core previously fell back to separate SELECT/INSERT/UPDATE sequences.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: Upsert::$key property type changed from string to string[]; third-party drivers that access $this->key directly as a string must update to treat it as an array.
  • New API: Upsert::key() now accepts string|array, enabling composite unique/primary key constraints in upsert queries across all three built-in database drivers (MySQL, PostgreSQL, SQLite).

Technical details

The Upsert base class (core/lib/Drupal/Core/Database/Query/Upsert.php) stored the key column as a bare string $key. It is now stored as string[] $key. The key() method signature changed from an untyped $field to string|array $field, normalized internally with (array) $field, so existing single-string callers remain unaffected.

All three built-in driver __toString() implementations were updated in parallel. Each now calls array_combine($insert_fields, $insert_fields) immediately after merging default and insert fields, making the array addressable by field name so that key columns can be removed with unset($insert_fields[$key]) inside a foreach ($this->key as $key) loop. The PostgreSQL and SQLite drivers build their ON CONFLICT (...) clause by mapping each key column through escapeField() and joining with commas. The MySQL driver similarly iterates the key array when excluding key columns from the ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE set list.

The SQLite driver (core/modules/sqlite/src/Driver/Database/sqlite/Upsert.php) also received a positional-index correctness fix: $insert_values = array_values($insert_values) is prepended to the blob-field processing loop so that numeric offsets align with $this->insertFields when values are passed as associative arrays.

A new kernel test testCompositeKeyUpsert() in core/tests/Drupal/KernelTests/Core/Database/UpsertTest.php exercises insert, conditional update, and same-name-different-age scenarios against a test_composite_primary table whose primary key spans (name, age).

Third-party database driver maintainers whose drivers override Upsert::__toString() and access $this->key directly as a string must update to treat it as string[]. A follow-up issue (#3549762) tracks a potential future deprecation of Merge queries in favour of Upsert.

Upgrade

Callers that pass a single string to Upsert::key() require no change:

// Still works as before.
$connection->upsert('my_table')
  ->key('id')
  ->fields(['id', 'value'])
  ->values([...])
  ->execute();

To use composite keys, pass an array:

$connection->upsert('my_table')
  ->key(['col_a', 'col_b'])
  ->fields(['col_a', 'col_b', 'value'])
  ->values([...])
  ->execute();

Third-party database drivers that override Upsert::__toString() and access $this->key as a string must update to iterate it as an array. A typical update looks like:

// Before:
unset($insert_fields[$this->key]);
$query .= ' ON CONFLICT (' . $this->connection->escapeField($this->key) . ') DO UPDATE SET ...';

// After:
foreach ($this->key as $key) {
    unset($insert_fields[$key]);
}
$keys = array_map(fn($k) => $this->connection->escapeField($k), $this->key);
$query .= ' ON CONFLICT (' . implode(', ', $keys) . ') DO UPDATE SET ...';

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 7, 2015
  • Committed: April 24, 2026 (10 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • amateescu

Collaboration

The issue was opened in 2015 but closed immediately because SQLite lacked real UPSERT support. After the minimum SQLite version was raised, amateescu reopened it in 2020; the patch then sat dormant for years. In 2025, two design discussions shaped the final commit. mondrake proposed overloading key() to accept string|array rather than adding a separate keyColumns() method, arguing this keeps the API simpler for callers who already use single-field upserts; amateescu agreed and updated the MR accordingly. A parallel debate with "ghost of drupal past", who argued that Upsert should be folded into Merge rather than kept as a separate API, was ultimately set aside by amateescu and mondrake, who chose to keep both classes and deferred any Merge evolution to a separate issue. longwave then extracted a minimal MR (MR!15248) focused strictly on the composite-key change, stripping out ancillary refactors, which was the version alexpott committed.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3577671: Modernize locale file handling
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 15 files, +514/−199 · php +512/−199 · yml +2
  • Diff: db002cb
  • Discussion: #3577671 · 5 contributors · 43 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

Six procedural functions in the locale module that handled translation file operations have been replaced by a proper service and typed value objects. Site owners with custom code that calls any of the deprecated locale file functions will see deprecation notices starting in Drupal 11.4.0, and must update before Drupal 13.0.0. There is no user-facing behavior change.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: Six procedural locale file functions in locale.bulk.inc and locale.batch.inc deprecated in favor of LocaleFileManager service and LocaleFile value object, removed in drupal:13.0.0.
  • New API: New LocaleFileManager service, LocaleFile typed value object, RemoteFileInfo value object, and RemoteFileStatus backed enum added under Drupal\locale\File\.

Technical details

The locale module historically passed translation file metadata around as loosely-typed stdClass objects and exposed file operations as bare procedural functions in locale.bulk.inc and locale.batch.inc. This change introduces four new classes under Drupal\locale\File\ and deprecates the six procedural functions.

New classes:

  • LocaleFile — a typed final class value object replacing ad-hoc stdClass instances. Its constructor takes filename, uri, hash, timestamp, langcode, project, and version. The static factory LocaleFile::createFromPath($filename, $filepath, $langcodeOverride) performs the regex-based filename parsing (previously embedded in locale_translate_file_attach_properties()) to extract project name, version, and language code from filenames matching {project}-{version}.{langcode}.po or {project}.{langcode}.po. The regex was explicitly moved to createFromPath() rather than the constructor, following a review observation from alexpott that running it on every construction was unnecessary and caused test failures.
  • LocaleFileManager — a new injectable service (registered in locale.services.yml as Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFileManager). It takes LocaleProjectStorageInterface, FileSystemInterface, ClientFactory, ClientInterface, LoggerChannelFactoryInterface, and MessengerInterface as constructor dependencies and exposes: getInterfaceTranslationFiles(), deleteTranslationFiles(), checkRemoteFileStatus(), and downloadTranslationSource().
  • RemoteFileInfo — a value object returned by checkRemoteFileStatus() carrying a RemoteFileStatus $status, nullable int $lastModified, and nullable string $location (set when a redirect occurred).
  • RemoteFileStatus — a backed int enum with cases Success = 1, Error = 0, Missing = 404.

Deprecated functions (all deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0, change record https://www.drupal.org/node/3577675):

  • locale_translate_get_interface_translation_files() → LocaleFileManager::getInterfaceTranslationFiles()
  • locale_translate_file_create() → new LocaleFile()
  • locale_translate_file_attach_properties() → LocaleFile::createFromPath()
  • locale_translate_delete_translation_files() → LocaleFileManager::deleteTranslationFiles()
  • locale_translation_http_check() → LocaleFileManager::checkRemoteFileStatus()
  • locale_translation_download_source() → LocaleFileManager::downloadTranslationSource()

All six deprecated functions are updated to thin @trigger_error wrappers that delegate to the new service or class. Internal callers in install.core.inc, locale.module, LocaleSource, LocaleEventSubscriber, and TranslationStatusForm are updated to call the service or LocaleFile directly.

Upgrade

Replace calls to the deprecated procedural functions with the new service and value objects:

// Before:
$files = locale_translate_get_interface_translation_files($projects, $langcodes);

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFileManager;
$files = \Drupal::service(LocaleFileManager::class)->getInterfaceTranslationFiles($projects, $langcodes);

// Before:
$file = locale_translate_file_create($filepath);

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFile;
use Drupal\locale\LocaleSource;
$hash = hash_file(LocaleSource::LOCAL_FILE_HASH_ALGO, $filepath);
$file = new LocaleFile(basename($filepath), $filepath, $hash, filemtime($filepath));

// Before:
$file = locale_translate_file_attach_properties($file, $options);

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFile;
$file = LocaleFile::createFromPath($filename, $uri, $options['langcode'] ?? NULL);

// Before:
locale_translate_delete_translation_files($projects, $langcodes);

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFileManager;
\Drupal::service(LocaleFileManager::class)->deleteTranslationFiles($projects, $langcodes);

// Before:
$result = locale_translation_http_check($uri);

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFileManager;
use Drupal\locale\File\RemoteFileStatus;
$remoteFileInfo = \Drupal::service(LocaleFileManager::class)->checkRemoteFileStatus($uri);
// $remoteFileInfo->status is a RemoteFileStatus enum: Success, Error, or Missing
// $remoteFileInfo->lastModified and $remoteFileInfo->location replace the old array keys

// Before:
$file = locale_translation_download_source($source_file, 'translations://');

// After:
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFile;
use Drupal\locale\File\LocaleFileManager;
use Drupal\locale\LocaleSource;
$hash = hash_file(LocaleSource::LOCAL_FILE_HASH_ALGO, $source_file->uri);
$locale_file = new LocaleFile($source_file->filename, $source_file->uri, $hash);
$file = \Drupal::service(LocaleFileManager::class)->downloadTranslationSource($locale_file, 'translations://');

Code that reads $file->info['version'] from a file object returned by these functions must be updated to read $file->version directly (a public property on LocaleFile).

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 7, 2026
  • First commit: April 10, 2026 (1 month later)
  • Last commit: April 24, 2026 (13 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • catch (Third and Grove)

Collaboration

berdir provided an early architectural review proposing the value-object approach and a scoping suggestion to keep the change manageable by using public properties. alexpott caught a critical structural issue: the filename-parsing regex had been moved into the LocaleFile constructor, causing it to run far more often than it did previously (only two call sites before the refactor, many more after). This introduced a test failure. nicxvan responded by moving the regex to the createFromPath() static factory and using plain new LocaleFile() construction everywhere else.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3366561: Support preloading of fonts in the libraries API
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 20 files, +159/−95 · php +138/−78 · yml +21/−2 · twig +0/−15
  • Diff: 26b9607
  • Discussion: #3366561 · 11 contributors · 60 comments
  • Followers: 51

Summary

Drupal's libraries API now supports a first-class fonts key in .libraries.yml files, allowing themes and modules to declare font preloading directly alongside their CSS and JS. When preload: true is set on a font entry, Drupal automatically emits a <link rel="preload" as="font"> tag in the HTML head. This replaces ad-hoc workarounds such as Olivero's hand-crafted Twig template and Navigation module's manual html_head_link attachment. Umami also gains font preloading for the first time. The practical effect is that browsers can begin fetching font files earlier, reducing the rendering delay caused by fonts that previously only started loading after CSS was downloaded and parsed.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor and BigPipeResponseAttachmentsProcessor constructors require a new FileUrlGeneratorInterface argument; omitting it is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and will be required in drupal:12.0.0.
  • New API: New AssetResolverInterface::getFontAssets() method and fonts: key in .libraries.yml for declaring font preloading.

Technical details

The problem was that font preloading had no structured place in the libraries system, forcing themes like Olivero to maintain a separate preload.twig template (with hardcoded paths) included from html.html.twig, and Navigation to manually build html_head_link arrays in PHP.

The fix adds fonts as a recognized top-level key alongside js and css in LibraryDiscoveryParser::buildByExtension(). The parser now defaults each library to 'fonts' => [] and iterates over ['js', 'css', 'fonts'] uniformly. Each font entry supports a preload: true flag.

A new getFontAssets(AttachedAssetsInterface $assets, ?LanguageInterface $language = NULL): array method is added to AssetResolverInterface and implemented in AssetResolver. It follows the same caching pattern as getCssAssets() and getJsAssets(), keying the persistent cache entry (library_info tag) on active theme, language, and the resolved library list via a fonts: prefix. Because the font library list is a subset of all attached libraries and typically changes less often than the full CSS set, the cache hit rate is higher.

HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor::processAttachments() calls getFontAssets() and, for each font with preload: true, pushes a <link rel="preload" as="font" type="font/<ext>" crossorigin="anonymous"> entry onto $attached['html_head_link']. URL generation uses FileUrlGeneratorInterface::generate() so that paths resolve correctly on non-root pages. FileUrlGeneratorInterface is injected as a new constructor argument on both HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor and BigPipeResponseAttachmentsProcessor; omitting it is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0.

Concrete conversions in the diff: Olivero's preload.twig template and its {% include %} line in html.html.twig are deleted; four font paths move to a fonts: block in olivero.libraries.yml. Navigation removes the manual html_head_link build from NavigationBlock::doBuildNavigation() and declares inter-var.woff2 in internal.navigation library. Umami adds three Source Sans Pro and one Scope One font to the global library.

Early-hints (103 responses) support is left for the follow-up issue #3570909.

Upgrade

Any code that constructs HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor or BigPipeResponseAttachmentsProcessor directly (outside of the service container) must add FileUrlGeneratorInterface as the last constructor argument. The service definitions in core.services.yml and big_pipe.services.yml are already updated.

// Before (deprecated since drupal:11.4.0):
new HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor(
  $assetResolver, $configFactory, $cssRenderer, $jsRenderer,
  $requestStack, $renderer, $moduleHandler, $languageManager
);

// After (required from drupal:12.0.0):
new HtmlResponseAttachmentsProcessor(
  $assetResolver, $configFactory, $cssRenderer, $jsRenderer,
  $requestStack, $renderer, $moduleHandler, $languageManager,
  $fileUrlGenerator   // \Drupal\Core\File\FileUrlGeneratorInterface
);

To declare font preloading in a theme or module library, add a fonts: block to the relevant .libraries.yml entry:

my-library:
  version: VERSION
  css:
    base:
      css/fonts.css: {}
  fonts:
    fonts/my-font-regular.woff2:
      preload: true
    fonts/my-font-bold.woff2:
      preload: true

The path is relative to the extension root, matching the URL that appears in the CSS @font-face declaration. External URLs are also accepted as keys.

Themes that previously preloaded fonts via a hand-maintained Twig template or a hook_preprocess_page() html_head_link attachment can remove that code and use the fonts: key instead.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 13, 2023
  • Committed: April 23, 2026 (2 years and 6 commits later)

Key contributors

  • catch (Third and Grove)
  • vbouchet (Acquia)
  • rikki_iki (PreviousNext)
  • berdir (MD Systems GmbH)
  • finnsky
  • grimreaper
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)

Collaboration

The API shape was debated across several comments. rikki_iki (comment #5) proposed using separate named top-level entries (preload:, preconnect:) rather than a generic links: block to reduce misuse risk, and catch agreed, eventually settling on a fonts: key with a preload: true flag per entry. berdir raised a practical concern about cache-busting: because the preload URL and the CSS @font-face URL must match exactly, and Drupal does not append a version string to font URLs, keeping them in sync is the author's responsibility. grimreaper provided code review that led to at least one test improvement catch had initially declined, and flagged the question of Single Directory Components; that was deferred to follow-up issue #3586470. finnsky made the first external commit, fixing PHPCS issues and adding Navigation support, which surfaced the path-resolution bug that led catch to introduce FileUrlGeneratorInterface.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3536964: run-tests.sh - segregate command line parsing and use Symfony Console classes
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +298/−0 · php +294 · yml +4
  • Diff: 45c8034
  • Discussion: #3536964 · 6 contributors · 65 comments
  • Followers: 9

Summary

The run-tests.sh test runner script now uses Symfony Console's argument-parsing infrastructure instead of a hand-rolled parsing function. A new Configuration class centralizes all command-line option handling, improving readability and moving the script closer to becoming a proper Symfony Console application command. For CI operators the most visible changes are: a substantially improved --help output (automatically generated from the option definitions), the --sqlite ':memory:' option now working (the old restriction is gone since #3515347 eliminated sub-process spawning), and the --php option being deprecated and silently ignored. PHP binary detection now uses PhpExecutableFinder from symfony/process.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • Deprecation: --php option in run-tests.sh is now deprecated and silently ignored; PHP binary detection is handled automatically via PhpExecutableFinder.

Technical details

All changes are in core/scripts/run-tests.sh and the new core/tests/Drupal/TestTools/TestRunner/Configuration.php.

Previously, run-tests.sh contained simpletest_script_parse_args(), a hand-written function that iterated $_SERVER['argv'], maintained a flat $args array of defaults, and shared it as a global throughout the script. Every function accessed global $args and then individual keys.

The new Configuration final singleton class lives in the Drupal\TestTools\TestRunner namespace. All options and their defaults are declared once in Configuration::commandLineDefinition() using Symfony Console's InputDefinition, InputOption, and InputArgument objects. The static factory Configuration::createFromCommandLine() wraps the raw argv in ArgvInput, validates --concurrency, splits the comma-separated tests and types values into arrays, and stores the result in the singleton. Access throughout the script is via Config::get('option-name'), Config::set('option-name', $value), and Config::getTests(). Functions that previously accepted array $args as a parameter (dump_tests_sequence(), dump_bin_tests_sequence()) now call Config::get() directly and drop the parameter.

simpletest_script_init() is simplified: autoloader setup is moved to the top of the script (the ClassLoader instance is passed in as a parameter), and the entire block that guessed the PHP binary from the $_ and SUDO_COMMAND environment variables is removed. The startup banner now calls (new PhpExecutableFinder())->find() inline. The --php option remains accepted so existing scripts do not break, but its value is never read and it is documented as deprecated.

simpletest_script_setup_test_run_results_storage() loses its $new boolean parameter; the caller always creates storage now.

simpletest_script_help() drops the full hand-written option listing and instead calls DescriptorHelper::describe() against the InputDefinition, automatically producing consistent formatted help. Its signature changes to accept InputDefinition $input_definition, string $script_basename, and ConsoleOutput $console_output instead of reading globals.

A companion change in .gitlab-ci.yml sets CI_NODE_INDEX: 1 and CI_NODE_TOTAL: 1 explicitly for the Build job, since those values are now required by the --ci-parallel-node-total and --ci-parallel-node-index option defaults.

Help text is also updated: the :memory: restriction on --sqlite is removed (resolved by #3515347), and references to @group annotations are replaced with #[Group()] attributes (see #3556580). The issue was explicitly blocked on #3515347 landing first, because that change removed the internal --test-id and --execute-test options and simplified the arguments this MR had to cover.

Upgrade

If your CI scripts pass --php to run-tests.sh, that argument is now a no-op and can safely be removed:

# Before
php ./core/scripts/run-tests.sh --php "$(which php)" --url http://example.com/ --all

# After
php ./core/scripts/run-tests.sh --url http://example.com/ --all

If your CI job uses --ci-parallel-node-total or --ci-parallel-node-index without supplying the corresponding CI_NODE_INDEX / CI_NODE_TOTAL environment variables, set them explicitly (or pass the values directly on the command line) to avoid an argument-without-value error.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: July 19, 2025
  • First commit: April 13, 2026 (8 months later)
  • Last commit: April 24, 2026 (10 days and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • mondrake
  • jonathan1055
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • amateescu

Collaboration

The issue was blocked for several months waiting on #3515347. Once that landed, jonathan1055 unblocked it and tested the MR in a real contrib CI pipeline using the Scheduler project's test suite, sharing pipeline links and comparing logs with the non-patched version. This surfaced a concrete bug: the startup banner was printing /usr/bin/sudo as the PHP binary path instead of the actual PHP executable. mondrake traced the root cause to the old environment-variable-sniffing logic and replaced it with PhpExecutableFinder::find() from symfony/process. jonathan1055 then retested and confirmed the fix. He also manually tested --list, --list-files, --help, and --class, which prompted clarifications in the help text around fully-qualified class names and escaped backslashes on the command line.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3581109: Move HelpSearch to Search sub-module
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 19 files, +233/−308 · php +223/−304 · yml +10/−4
  • Diff: bc0673e
  • Discussion: #3581109 · 6 contributors · 34 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A new search_help core sub-module now owns the Help search plugin and its related database table. Previously, the help module contained a search plugin that depended on the optional search module, creating an unwanted hard coupling. Sites that rely on searching help topics must install the new search_help module to retain that functionality. For most sites the help module itself is unchanged.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Database update: No schema change at runtime; the help_search_items table definition moves from help_schema() to search_help_schema(), so the table only exists when search_help is installed.
  • API change: HelpSearch renamed to SearchHelpSearch and moved to Drupal\search_help\Plugin\Search; HelpSectionManager::setSearchManager() and the clearCachedDefinitions() override are removed.
  • New API: New search_help core module provides the help_search search plugin and the help_search_items table schema, previously owned by the help module.

Technical details

The HelpSearch search plugin, the help_search_items database table schema, and the hook implementations that kept the search index in sync were all bundled inside the help module despite depending on the optional search module. This change extracts them into a dedicated search_help sub-module that declares explicit dependencies on drupal:help and search:search.

The plugin class is renamed from HelpSearch to SearchHelpSearch and moved from Drupal\help\Plugin\Search to Drupal\search_help\Plugin\Search. The help_schema() hook (defining the help_search_items table) is removed from help.install and replaced by search_help_schema() in the new module's install file.

HelpSectionManager::setSearchManager() and the clearCachedDefinitions() override (which called markForReindex() when the search plugin existed) are both removed. The plugin.manager.help_section service definition no longer injects the search manager via a calls directive. The index-rebuild trigger is instead handled by a new hook_rebuild implementation in SearchHelpHooks, which uses constructor injection with #[Autowire] for SearchPluginManager and CachedDiscoveryClearerInterface rather than \Drupal::service() calls. The hook implementations for modules_installed, modules_uninstalled, themes_installed, and themes_uninstalled that updated updateTopicList() and updateIndexState() are also moved from the help module's hooks class into this new SearchHelpHooks class. Several methods in the plugin class (getType(), execute(), updateIndex(), indexClear(), updateTopicList(), updateIndexState(), markForReindex(), indexStatus(), removeItemsFromIndex()) gain explicit return type declarations, removing the corresponding PHPStan baseline suppressions. The constructor is modernized to use promoted properties.

The commit was initially landed, reverted due to apparent CI slowness, then re-landed after the slowness was traced to a separate race-condition issue (#3496257) unrelated to this change.

Upgrade

If you have the Help search plugin enabled (visible at /admin/config/search/pages), install the new search_help module to retain that functionality:

drush en search_help

If your custom code references the old class or namespace, update it:

// Before
use Drupal\help\Plugin\Search\HelpSearch;

// After
use Drupal\search_help\Plugin\Search\SearchHelpSearch;

If your code calls HelpSectionManager::setSearchManager(), remove those calls. The method no longer exists.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 24, 2026
  • Committed: April 22, 2026 (29 days and 3 commits later)

Key contributors

  • quietone (PreviousNext)
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • dcam
  • godotislate

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3443428: Whitespace in Views exposed filters returns incorrect results
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal bug report
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +24/−0 · php +24
  • Diff: 4bf4ca8
  • Discussion: #3443428 · 5 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

When a user typed a search term with leading or trailing spaces into a Views exposed text filter (for example "foo " instead of "foo"), the view returned no results. The fix trims whitespace from the filter value before it reaches the database query, so those searches now return the expected results. The change was committed to both main and 11.x.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The bug was in core/modules/views/src/Plugin/views/filter/FilterString.php. When an exposed input was accepted, the raw string value (including any surrounding whitespace) was passed directly to the query, causing an exact-match failure against stored values that have no whitespace.

The fix adds an acceptExposedInput() override to FilterString. After delegating to the parent method, if the parent accepted the input and $this->value is a string, it calls trim($this->value) in place before returning. This ensures the sanitized value is used in query().

A test case was added to FilterStringTest::testFilterStringGroupedExposedEqual() confirming that setting the exposed input to ' Ringo ' (with surrounding spaces) returns the row for 'Ringo'.

When committing, xjm explicitly evaluated backport safety: the behavior change (returning results that previously returned none) was considered minor-only acceptable, but the new method override in FilterString could conflict with contrib or custom subclasses that already override acceptExposedInput(). She committed to both main and 11.x anyway, characterizing it as a slightly disruptive bugfix.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 25, 2024
  • Committed: April 23, 2026 (1 year and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • gaurav_manerkar (EPAM)
  • joewhitsitt
  • smustgrave (Mobomo)
  • xjm (Zoocha)

Collaboration

xjm manually reproduced the bug step by step before committing, documented her test procedure in a comment, ran the test-only CI job to verify the new assertion caught the regression, and then explicitly reasoned through patch-safety before deciding to backport to 11.x alongside main.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3583795: Change the new CLI entry point to “dr”
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: main
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3583795 · 9 contributors · 23 comments
  • Followers: 15

Summary

The new built-in Drupal CLI tool, being developed in #3453474, was originally named drupal (invoked as vendor/bin/drupal). This issue reached consensus to rename the official command to dr before the CLI becomes widely adopted. The shorter name avoids overloading the project name, reduces cognitive overhead, requires no aliasing, and follows the pattern established by two-character CLIs like wp, gh, ng, and az. The rename was applied in the merge request for #3453474, with the old drupal entry point kept as a deprecated wrapper pointing to dr.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The CLI entry point lives in core/scripts/ and is declared as a Composer bin, so it is copied into vendor/bin/ under the same filename. Because Composer provides no mechanism to alias or rename binned scripts, the rename requires renaming core/scripts/drupal to core/scripts/dr and providing a separate core/scripts/drupal shim that emits a deprecation notice and delegates to dr via return include __DIR__ . '/dr'. The deprecation window proposed in the thread is drupal:11.4.0 removed in drupal:13.0.0. The actual file changes are tracked in the parent issue #3453474; this issue served purely as the naming decision record. A minor trade-off noted in the thread is that Drupal.org issue search requires at least three characters, so dr cannot be searched directly, though that limitation is expected to disappear as core issues migrate to GitLab.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 9, 2026

Key contributors

  • xmacinfo (xMac info)
  • amateescu
  • mradcliffe (Kosada)
  • phenaproxima (Acquia)
  • rymo
  • mondrake
  • jwilson3 (Bluespark)
  • longwave (Full Fat Things)
  • dww (3281d Consulting)

Collaboration

jwilson3 wrote the comment (#16558533) that turned the thread: a detailed comparison of two-character CLI tools (wp, gh, ng, az, go) versus longer-named alternatives, arguing that dr is unique in vendor/bin/ namespace (no competitors starting with d), easy to type one-handed, and signals intentional CLI maturity rather than a convenience alias. Issue author xmacinfo immediately endorsed it and invited jwilson3 to update the issue summary, which jwilson3 did, reframing the whole proposal around dr. mradcliffe then worked out the concrete implementation approach: a deprecated core/scripts/drupal wrapper calling return include __DIR__ . '/dr', a pattern confirmed by longwave's suggestion in comment #16559208.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #2473041: Restructure node access grant behavior into the node access handler
item.description:
• Project: Drupal Core
  • Type: Normal task
  • Version: 11.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +92/−12 · php +88/−11 · yml +4/−1
  • Diff: 26f4179
  • Discussion: #2473041 · 8 contributors · 64 comments
  • Followers: 14

Summary

The procedural node_access_grants() function, a holdover from Drupal 7 kept in node.module, has been moved into a new injectable service NodeGrantsHelper. The function itself is now deprecated and will be removed in Drupal 13. This is an internal refactoring with no change in behavior for site owners, but module authors who call node_access_grants() directly need to update their code.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: NodeGrantDatabaseStorage and NodeAccessGrantsCacheContext each gain a new optional ?NodeGrantsHelper $nodeGrantsHelper constructor argument, deprecated without it in 11.4.0 and required in 12.0.0
  • Deprecation: node_access_grants() procedural function deprecated in drupal:11.4.0 and removed in drupal:13.0.0; use \Drupal::service('Drupal\node\NodeGrantsHelper')->nodeAccessGrants() instead
  • New API: New NodeGrantsHelper service (Drupal\node\NodeGrantsHelper) with nodeAccessGrants(string $operation, AccountInterface $account): array method

Technical details

The core problem was that node_access_grants() — which invokes hook_node_grants and hook_node_grants_alter and returns the merged grants array — was a procedural function in node.module with no OOP equivalent. Moving it to NodeAccessControlHandler was the original plan, but that would have introduced a circular dependency: NodeGrantDatabaseStorage (which calls node_access_grants() three times internally) is itself a dependency of NodeAccessControlHandler.

The resolution was to introduce a dedicated NodeGrantsHelper class (core/modules/node/src/NodeGrantsHelper.php) with a single public method nodeAccessGrants(string $operation, AccountInterface $account): array. This class takes ModuleHandlerInterface as its only constructor dependency and contains the exact logic previously in node_access_grants(). It is registered as the service Drupal\node\NodeGrantsHelper in node.services.yml.

With the new service available, all internal callers were updated:

  • NodeGrantDatabaseStorage gains a new ?NodeGrantsHelper $nodeGrantsHelper constructor parameter (deprecated without it in 11.4.0, required in 12.0.0), replacing three direct calls to node_access_grants().
  • NodeAccessGrantsCacheContext gains the same new optional constructor parameter under the same deprecation schedule, replacing one call in checkNodeGrants().
  • The Views NodeStatus filter's query() method uses \Drupal::service(NodeGrantsHelper::class)->nodeAccessGrants() as a static call since it cannot easily be injected there.
  • node_access_grants() itself is deprecated in drupal:11.4.0, removed in drupal:13.0.0, and now simply delegates to the new service.

The related issue #3566536 was a prior prerequisite that was resolved before this one could proceed.

Upgrade

If your code calls node_access_grants() directly, replace it with the new service method:

// Before
$grants = node_access_grants($operation, $account);

// After
$grants = \Drupal::service(\Drupal\node\NodeGrantsHelper::class)->nodeAccessGrants($operation, $account);

// Or with proper injection
public function __construct(
  protected readonly \Drupal\node\NodeGrantsHelper $nodeGrantsHelper,
) {}

$grants = $this->nodeGrantsHelper->nodeAccessGrants($operation, $account);

If your code constructs NodeGrantDatabaseStorage or NodeAccessGrantsCacheContext directly, pass the new NodeGrantsHelper service as the final constructor argument to avoid a deprecation warning:

// NodeGrantDatabaseStorage — pass NodeGrantsHelper as the 5th argument
// NodeAccessGrantsCacheContext — pass NodeGrantsHelper as the 3rd argument

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 17, 2015
  • Committed: April 22, 2026 (11 years and 2 commits later)

Key contributors

  • xjm (xjm)
  • acbramley (PreviousNext)
  • joey-santiago
  • alexpott (Acro Commerce)
  • steven jones (ComputerMinds)
  • nicxvan (nLightened Development LLC)
  • godotislate

Collaboration

steven jones picked this issue back up after a decade of dormancy, noting that node_access_grants() was called in only five places in core and the work seemed straightforward. He quickly discovered the circular dependency problem and proposed the third-service solution in comment #16463437, reasoning that NodeGrantsHelper could eventually serve as the seam for replacing hook-based grants with a plugin-based system. acbramley argued (comment #16463496) for keeping the scope minimal — just extracting the function into a service without rearchitecting anything — rather than reviving the broader restructuring from the 2015 issue summary. nicxvan guided the naming and service definition conventions, and godotislate landed the final reviews and commits to both main and 11.x, also bumping the removal target from Drupal 12 to Drupal 13 because node_access_grants() is a public function used by contrib (comment #16557916).

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
